user_story: As a project manager, I want to be able to start a new project by providing necessary inputs such as the Stop Word file and Weight Option (TF-IDF, Okapi LTU), so that I can initiate my project in either 'Project Mode' or 'Trace' mode. Additionally, I should have the option to cancel the process at any time before the project is created.
true_user_story: The START NEW PROJECT function shall allow the user to create a new project.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be prompted to enter the new project name when starting a new project, so that I can accurately name my project and proceed with the setup process.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the new project name.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be prompted for the new project name, low level document path, high level document path, and RTM file path when starting a new project, so that all necessary information is collected upfront and the project can be created accurately.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the low level document path.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be prompted to provide the new project name, high level document path, low level document path, and RTM file path when starting a new project, so that all necessary information is collected for project initialization.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the high level document.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted with options to select an IR Method from the list: Vector-Space Retrieval, Keyword Extraction, and Keyword Extraction-IDF, so that I can choose the appropriate method for my project.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to select an IR Method from the following list: Vector-Space Retrieval Keyword Extraction Keyword Extraction-IDF

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to select a Vocabulary Base of either 'Both Documents' or 'Low Level Document Only', with the default value set to 'Both Documents', so that I can customize my project settings according to my needs.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to select a Vocabulary Base of either “Both Documents” or “Low Level Document Only”.  The default value is “Both Documents”.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to select from the list of Weight Options (TF-IDF, Okapi, LTU) when starting a new project, so that I can customize my information retrieval method based on my specific needs.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide Weight Option from the following list: TF-IDF (Default) Okapi LTU

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to select a Weight Threshold when choosing an IR Method of 'Keyword Extraction' or 'Keyword Extraction-IDF', so that the system can accurately process my project data.
true_user_story: If the user has selected an IR Method of Keyword Extraction or Keyword Extraction-IDF, the user must also select a Weight Threshold.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to provide a Thesaurus filename and a Stop Word filename when starting a new project, so that I can customize the text processing options according to my needs.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide a Thesaurus Filename.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to provide a Thesaurus Filename and a Stop Word file when starting a new project, so that I can customize the project settings according to my needs.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide the Stop Word file.

user_story: As a project creator, I want to be optionally prompted to provide a Feedback Method during the project creation process, so that I can choose the appropriate method for my project needs.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide a Feedback Method.  “Standard Rochio” is currently the only option.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to cancel the process at any time before the project is created, so that I can return to the previous state without losing any progress.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to cancel the process and return to the previous state at any time before the project is created.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be able to create a new project with optional stop word file, thesaurus filename, and feedback method selection, so that I can customize the project settings according to my needs.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to create the project using the above input.  The project shall be placed in “Trace” mode.

user_story: As a user, I want to load an existing project in its last saved state with all associated mode options, so that I can continue working on my project from where I left off.
true_user_story: Since the majority of projects are not completed in a single setting, then it is critical to provide the user the option to load an existing project to continue working on the project.  Load Project feature will provide the user the capacity to open an existing project in the last saved state with all the associated mode options. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to enter a name for a new project when attempting to load an RTM project, so that I can create a new project with a unique and descriptive name.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to select a project name from a list of saved projects.  Project names are obtained by scanning the saved projects directory.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to provide a Feedback Method ('Standard Rochio') when loading a project, so that my feedback preferences are considered during the project load process.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to select a feedback method.  The only option is “Standard Rochio”.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to cancel the LOAD PROJECT action at any time before the project is loaded, so that I can return to my previous activity without any interruptions or loss of progress.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to cancel the LOAD PROJECT action and return to the previous activity.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to cancel the LOAD PROJECT action and return to the previous activity, so that I can avoid loading an unwanted project and continue with my current task without interruption.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to load the project once the project name and feedback method is selected.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be able to load an RTM assessment process into my project using the LOAD RTM function, so that I can trace and manage requirements effectively.
true_user_story: Similar in functionality to the START NEW PROJECT function, the LOAD RTM function collects project parameters from the user and initializes the project.  A project created via the LOAD RTM function may be in either “trace” or “assess” mode.  Assess mode is usually used when the user is provided a RTM and needs to evaluate its accuracy, completeness, and satisfaction level.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be prompted to enter the new project name when using the LOAD RTM function, so that I can accurately initialize my project with the correct title.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the new project name.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be prompted for the new project name, low level document path, high level document path, and RTM file path when creating a new project, so that all necessary information is collected before proceeding with the project setup.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the low level document path.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be prompted to provide the high level document path and RTM file path when loading an RTM, so that the system can accurately initialize the project in either 'trace' or 'assess' mode.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the high level document path.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to provide the high-level document path and RTM file path when loading an RTM, so that the system can accurately locate and process the necessary files.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the RTM file path.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to select an IR Method from the list including Vector-Space Retrieval, Keyword Extraction, and Keyword Extraction-IDF, so that I can choose the appropriate method for my needs while loading the RTM file.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to select an IR Method from the following list: Vector-Space Retrieval Keyword Extraction Keyword Extraction-IDF

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to select a Vocabulary Base of either “Both Documents” or “Low Level Document Only”, with the default value being “Both Documents”, so that I can customize my project settings according to my needs.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to select a Vocabulary Base of either “Both Documents” or “Low Level Document Only”.  The default value is “Both Documents”.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to select a Weight Option from the list (TF-IDF (Default), Okapi, LTU) when loading an RTM file, so that I can customize the weighting method for my keyword extraction process.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide “Weight Option” from the following list: TF-IDF (Default) Okapi LTU

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to select a Weight Threshold when choosing an IR Method of Keyword Extraction or Keyword Extraction-IDF, so that the system can accurately process my input.
true_user_story: If the user has selected an IR Method of “Keyword Extraction” or “Keyword Extraction-IDF”, the user must also select a “Weight Threshold”.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to provide a Thesaurus filename and a Stop Word filename when loading an RTM project, so that I can customize my project parameters according to my specific needs.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide a “Thesaurus” filename.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to provide a Thesaurus filename and a Stop Word filename when loading RTM, so that I can customize the text processing parameters according to my specific needs.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide the “Stop Word” filename.

user_story: As a user, I want to optionally provide a Feedback Method (currently only "Standard Rochio" is available), so that I can customize my feedback process according to my preferences.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide a “Feedback Method”.  “Standard Rochio” is currently the only option.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to select either 'Trace' or 'Assess' mode when creating a project, with 'Trace' being the default option, so that I can specify whether I am provided an RTM and need to evaluate its accuracy, completeness, and satisfaction level.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide the “Project Mode” of either “Trace” or “Assess”.  “Trace” is the default mode.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to cancel the process at any time before the project is created, so that I can return to the previous state without losing any progress.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to cancel the process and return to the previous state at any time before the project is created.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be able to create a new project with default input fields and select either 'Trace' or 'Assess' mode, so that I can efficiently start the project in the desired mode.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to create the project using the above input.  The project shall be placed in specified “Project Mode”.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be able to save the current data and status of my project using the SAVE function, so that I can ensure all progress is retained and can be accessed later.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to activate the SAVE function to save the current data and status of the project.

user_story: As a user, I want RETRO to save all current data and project when activated, so that my work is not lost.
true_user_story: When activated RETRO will save all current data and project.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be able to close my current project without exiting the RETRO application, so that I can save my work and return to it later.
true_user_story: The CLOSE CURRENT PROJECT Function will allow the user to close a given project without exiting the RETRO application.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to close the current project without exiting the RETRO application, so that I can save my progress and return to it later.
true_user_story: The user shall be able close the current project.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to exit the RETRO application when I am finished working with it, so that I can properly close my session and ensure no further actions are taken within the application.
true_user_story: It is necessary to provide a mechanism to exit the RETRO application when the user is finished working with the application.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to exit the RETRO application when I am finished working with it, so that I can close the application and free up system resources.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to exit the application.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to view the current project’s XML file format when I select the SHOW XML function, so that I can see the list of high and low connections in XML format on a new screen.
true_user_story: The user may want to view the current project’s XML file format.  The SHOW XML function will provide the user a display of the current XML file.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be able to view the XML version of the RTM, so that I can ensure the data integrity and structure of my project’s documentation.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to view the XML version of the RTM.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be able to view a list of available reports after completing a trace or assess process, so that I can select and generate the report that best fits my needs.
true_user_story: After a project has completed either a trace or an assess process, then the user will need the capability to review the final results of the process in an easy to read format other than the XML file format that is automatically generated throughout the process.  The GENERATE REPORT function will provide the user a list of reports that the user may view.  The user will have the option to save the generated report or modify the design of the report. [1]

user_story: As a project manager, I want to generate an RTM report with text option from the Action menu, so that I can review the final results of the process in an easy-to-read format and save it for future reference.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “RTM report”

user_story: As a project manager, I want to generate and save the 'Children with no parents report', so that I can easily review and analyze the orphaned entities in our system.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Parent with no children report”

user_story: As a data analyst, I want to generate and save the 'Parents with no children with text report', so that I can identify parents without any associated children in the system.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Parents with no children with text report”

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be able to generate and save various reports such as 'Parents with no children', 'Children with no parents', 'RTM report', and 'Not satisfied requirements report', so that I can review the final results of the process in an easy-to-read format other than XML.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Children with no parents report”

user_story: As a project manager, I want to generate and save the 'Children with no parents with text report', so that I can review the final results of the process in an easy-to-read format other than the XML file format.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Children with no parents with text report”

user_story: As a project manager, I want to generate and save the "Not satisfied requirements with text report" so that I can easily review and share the final results of the process in an accessible format other than XML.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Not satisfied requirements report”

user_story: As a project manager, I want to generate and save the "Not satisfied requirements with text report" so that I can easily review and share the final results of the process in a readable format other than XML.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Not satisfied requirements with text report”

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to limit the display of low-level requirements or candidate requirement links, so that I can focus on the most relevant information without missing any important details.
true_user_story: “The user shall be able to limit the display of low-level requirements or candidate requirement links.” [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to specify whether the filter process applies to the selected high-level document element (LOCAL) or to all high-level document elements (GLOBAL), so that I can control the scope of the filtering process effectively.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to designate whether the filter process applies to the selected high level document element (LOCAL) or to all high level document elements (GLOBAL).

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be able to filter low-level requirements by setting a weight cutoff point between 0 and 1.0, so that I can focus on the most critical requirements and improve decision-making efficiency.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to filter the list of low-level requirements by setting a cutoff point for the weight between 0 and 1.0.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to designate the top N elements to be displayed, so that I can control which low-level requirements are shown based on my preferences.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to designate that the top N elements to be displayed.

user_story: As a user, I want to activate the Kill Hidden Links function, so that all elements not currently displayed are removed.
true_user_story: The user shall be able activate a KILL HIDDEN Links function to remove all elements not currently displayed.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to sort low-level documents by "Requirement ID", "Document Order", and "Weights" so that I can easily organize and view the text of low-level requirements according to my preferences.
true_user_story: The user will need the ability to view the text of low-level requirements.  The DISPLAY function will provide three different sort options to view the text of low-level requirements.  The user can sort the low-level requirements based upon the weight of linking relationships, original document order, or by requirement id. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to sort low-level documents by 'Requirement ID', 'Weights', and 'Document Order', so that I can easily organize and find specific information within the documents.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to sort the low level documents by “Requirement ID”

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be able to sort low-level documents by 'Weights', so that I can prioritize tasks based on their importance and dependencies.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to sort the low level documents by “Weights”

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to sort low-level documents by Document Order, Requirement ID, and Weights, so that I can efficiently organize and view the information in a manner that best suits my needs.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to sort the low level documents by “Document Order”

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to switch between Trace and Assess modes, so that I can accurately determine the success of the tracing process.
true_user_story: Since RETRO’s tracing and assessing modes are based upon algorithms that will allow processes to be improved as the system receives FEEDBACK from the user.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be able to quickly remove low-level requirements with a status of 'No Link', so that I can efficiently manage and clean up my project data based on the feedback received from the system.
true_user_story: The user shall select a low level requirement and identify the link status from the following list: Link No Link Default

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to load multiple projects simultaneously, so that I can continue working on different projects without having to close and reopen them each time.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to load multiple projects at the same time.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be able to open multiple projects simultaneously in their last saved states with all associated mode options, so that I can efficiently manage and compare different projects without having to exit the RETRO application.
true_user_story: The RETRO System shall allow multiple projects to be open at the same time

user_story: As a project manager, I want to import existing projects from text files into RETRO file format, so that I can easily manage and convert my legacy data into the new system.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to import projects.

user_story: As a developer, I want to be able to convert existing input text files into RETRO file format, so that I can seamlessly import projects into the system.
true_user_story: The RETRO System shall provide a mechanism to convert existing input text files into RETRO file format.  (Not implemented.)

user_story: As a user, I want the RETRO System to provide an easy way to view each individual requirement through the GUI interface, so that I can efficiently navigate and understand the system's functionalities without any confusion or difficulty.
true_user_story: The user shall use a GUI INTERFACE to work with RETRO.

user_story: As a user, I want to interact with RETRO using a GUI interface, so that I can efficiently work with the system without any crashes or spelling errors in labels and documentation.
true_user_story: The RETRO System shall provide a GUI mechanism for the user to interact with the RETRO System.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to enter keywords into a search text box to search within both high-level and low-level requirements, so that I can quickly locate the desired requirement.
true_user_story: The user is provided the capability via BROWSE mode to manually link low-level requirements to high-level requirement. In order to help the user to quickly locate the desired low-level requirement or high-level requirement, a KEYWORD SEARCH function is offered to the user. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to enter a keyword into a search text box to search within low-level requirements, so that I can quickly locate the desired requirement.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to enter a keyword into a search text box to search within low-level requirements and the system shall return matches.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to enter a keyword into a search text box to search within high-level requirements, so that I can quickly locate the desired high-level requirement and receive relevant matches.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to enter a keyword into a search text box to search within high-level requirements and the system shall return matches.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to manually select and delete recommended links in RETRO, so that I can ensure the accuracy of the linking process according to my needs.
true_user_story: There will be times when the user will not agree with RETRO’s list of candidate links.  Therefore, it is important to provide a mechanism for the user to manually change, create, or delete links between low and high level requirements.  BROWSE mode is the mechanism within RETRO that enables the user to perform MANUAL LINKING/TRACING. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to limit the display of low-level requirements or candidate requirement links, so that I can focus on the most relevant information and avoid clutter when performing manual linking/tracing in BROWSE mode.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to create and remove low-level requirements with high-level requirements.

user_story: As a project manager, I want RETRO to automatically generate a list of candidate links from high-level to low-level requirements, so that I can efficiently trace and manage requirement dependencies.
true_user_story: One of the purposes of RETRO is to aid the user in linking low-level requirements with high-level requirements.  TRACING functionality is the component of RETRO that automatically generates a list of candidate links of high to low-level requirements.

user_story: As a requirements analyst, I want RETRO to automatically generate candidate links between high-level and low-level requirements, so that I can efficiently evaluate and establish traceability in the requirement documentation.
true_user_story: RETRO shall provide a mechanism for the user to generate a trace on all requirements.

user_story: As a requirements analyst, I want RETRO to automatically generate candidate links between high-level and low-level requirements, so that I can efficiently evaluate and establish traceability across all requirements.
true_user_story: RETRO shall provide a mechanism for the user to generate a trace on a given requirement(s).

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to provide feedback on the tracing results, so that future tracing processes can be improved based on my input.
true_user_story: After the TRACING function is performed, then the results of the TRACING process will need to be made available to the user to view.  The TRACING RESULTS functionality will display a list of all candidate links of high to low-level requirements based on any given filtering criteria.  The user will be allowed to provide feedback on the TRACING RESULTS in order to improve future TRACING.

user_story: As a user, I want RETRO to display a list of candidate links based on tracing results, so that I can quickly identify the level of satisfaction for each link and provide feedback to improve future tracing.
true_user_story: RETRO shall display the list of candidate links based on the tracing and permit feedback, filtering, and display functionality.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be able to save the current status of the trace, so that I can resume my work later without losing any progress.
true_user_story: The SAVE TRACE function shall allow the user to save the trace result.

user_story: As a user, I want RETRO to provide an option to switch between "Trace" (default) and "Assess" modes, so that I can choose the appropriate mode for my trace analysis.
true_user_story: RETRO shall provide a mechanism to save the current status of trace.

user_story: As a project manager, I want the COMPLETING TRACE function to allow me to finish the tracing process, so that I can mark the project as completed and prevent any further changes.
true_user_story: The COMPLETING TRACE function shall allow user to finish the tracing process.

user_story: As a project team member, I want RETRO to save all current data and projects when activated, so that I can ensure no information is lost during the tracing process.
true_user_story: RETRO shall mark the project completed and not permit further changes.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to provide feedback on the TRACING RESULTS in order to improve future TRACING, so that the accuracy and completeness of the tracing can be continuously enhanced.
true_user_story: The user needs a mechanism that will help to determine whether the TRACING was successfully performed based on accuracy and completeness.  An accuracy test tells the user that the candidate links provided are correctly related to the high-level requirement.  Completeness test tells the user that all possible links that are listed in the candidate list are included and no low-level requirements are missing. [1]

user_story: As a requirements engineer, I want to be able to run an ACCURACY & COMPLETENESS check by performing a full trace on the requirements and comparing the IR method results with the given RTM results, so that I can ensure the accuracy and completeness of the requirements tracing process. This will help me identify any recommended links for improvement in the details contained in the RTM.
true_user_story: RETRO shall provide a mechanism for the user to be able to run an ACCURACY & COMPLETENESS check by performing a full trace on the requirements and comparing the IR method results to the given RTM. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to view a list of recommendations generated by RETRO’s TRACING process while in ASSESS mode, so that I can evaluate and provide feedback on whether the recommendations align with my results for the given RTM.
true_user_story: “The [user] will need to have a list of recommendations based on RETRO’s TRACING process.  SHOW RECOMMENDATION will display to the [user] a list of recommendation while in ASSESS mode.  The [user] will have the capability to provide FEEDBACK to the RETRO system on whether or not the [user] agrees with RETRO’s assessment of the linking between the low-level requirements to the high-level requirements.” [1]

user_story: As a user, I want RETRO to display/hide recommendations based on my request, so that I can control the visibility of suggestions while in ASSESS mode.
true_user_story: RETRO shall display/hide recommendations at the user’s request.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to see the satisfaction status ('Satisfied', 'Not Satisfied', or 'Partially Satisfied') of high-level requirements based on their linked low-level requirements, so that I can quickly assess the overall progress and satisfaction level of our project.
true_user_story: Satisfaction function shows how satisfied the high-level requirement is with the given set of low-level requirements. [4] The Satisfaction Analysis function is checking the level of percentage for a satisfaction of the linked result with the high and low level input files. Once final output is agreed, the list of high level requirement is updated with options like “Satisfied”, “Not Satisfied” and “Partially Satisfied”. The status of high level requirement is color coded. Unsatisfied result can be repeated until all the high levels has been satisfied stage.

user_story: As a user, I want RETRO to display the percentage level of satisfaction for each link when I run the Satisfaction Analysis function, so that I can quickly identify and address any unsatisfied links.
true_user_story: RETRO shall display to the user the percentage level of link. [1]

user_story: As a project manager, I want to be able to delete recommended links that are incorrectly linked while in ASSESS mode, so that I can maintain accurate and relevant requirement linkages.
true_user_story: While in ASSESS mode, the user will need a mechanism to allow the removal of all low-level requirements that are not linked to a high-level requirement.  REMOVE ALL NO LINKS function will provide an easy way for the user to quickly remove low-level requirements with status of “No Link”.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to select and delete recommended links, so that I can efficiently manage and clean up my data by removing unnecessary or outdated links.
true_user_story: RETRO shall remove all links marked as “No Link”

user_story: As a user in ASSESS mode, I want to be able to delete recommended links that I feel are not correctly linked, so that I can maintain accurate linking of requirements.
true_user_story: While in ASSESS mode, the user will need a mechanism that will allow the deletion of recommended links that the user feels are not correctly linked.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to delete recommended links that are incorrectly linked while in ASSESS mode, so that I can ensure the accuracy of the linking process.
true_user_story: RETRO shall permit the user to select a recommended link and delete it.

user_story: As a user, I want RETRO to color-code the satisfaction status of each candidate link based on its satisfaction level, so that I can quickly identify each link's satisfaction level using visual cues.
true_user_story: “In order to help the user to quickly identify the level of satisfaction of each candidate link, RETRO will need to provide a visual aid.  COLOR-CODED SATISFACTION STATUS function will color code each candidate link based on its satisfaction level.   The various colors will be a visual aid to the user to help quickly identify each links satisfaction level.” [1]

user_story: As a user of RETRO, I want each candidate link to be color-coded based on its satisfaction level, so that I can quickly identify the level of satisfaction for each link.
true_user_story: RETRO shall COLOR-CODE THE SATISFACTION STATUS of each given candidate link.
